reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology asks the practitioner to weigh Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 0:46; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “It's actually more of like a circadian rhythm peptides.” Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 19:07; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “It's on a cure-all that's going to fix everything.”
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 14:48; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “Again, when it comes to peptides, obviously long-term is unknown. There is the immunogenicity risk, meaning the immune system might create antibodies against it.”
- Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 14:11; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “the most commonly reported side effects have been mild and transient, brief headache, nausea, or slight dizziness after injection.”
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus:
Hunter (2026-03-29; dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a @ 2:54; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a/): “DCEP does not help you fall sleep, it helps with delta sleep.”
Taylor (2026-05-25; reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e @ 30:46; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e/): “DSEP isn't going help you fall asleep, it's just gonna help stay in your deep sleep realm longer.”
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement:
Newer 2026-03-30: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:21; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “I like starting around the 100 microgram mark and then going up to around 500 micro grams if needed.”
Older 2026-03-29: Hunter (2026-03-29; dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a @ 10:23; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a/): “The starting dose is going to be usually like 100 to 150 milligrams, or excuse me, micrograms, not milligrams. 150 micro grams, you can titrate up to 250 micro-gram.”

acute.dose: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 14:48; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “Now dosage protocols, the research doses, if you're doing IV, it's 25 nanomoles a kilogram per body weight.”
acute.route: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 9:49; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “there was an 1984 European trial, that was approximately 100 inpatients that received DSP intravenously during acute withdrawal.”

chronic.dose: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:21; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “the typical starting dose is 100 to 200 micrograms, and then you can increase up to 300 to 500 micro grams if needed.” Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:51; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “a lot of people take 125 or 150 micrograms” Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:51; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “I've even heard people as high, I haven't done this myself, people going up one milligram if you really need it.” Hunter (2026-03-29; dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a @ 10:23; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-how-to-use-it-to-enhance-deep-sleep--ae3f528a/): “The starting dose is going to be usually like 100 to 150 milligrams, or excuse me, micrograms, not milligrams. 150 micro grams, you can titrate up to 250 micro-gram.”
chronic.route: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:21; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “If you are listening to this, you will be doing it sub Q.”
chronic.frequency: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 16:25; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “500 micrograms subcutaneously, usually two to three hours before bed would be a good protocol.”
chronic.duration: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 14:48; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “if there is a chronic issue you're trying to heal, you can absolutely use for eight to 12 weeks at a time. You just might have to up the dose or cycle off to keep getting the benefits.”
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter (2026-03-30; dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6 @ 15:21; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/dsip-updated-dosing-sleep-and-opioid-withdrawal-research--a23e73c6/): “I like starting around the 100 microgram mark and then going up to around 500 micro grams if needed.”

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor (2026-05-25; reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e @ 29:08; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e/): “For women, sleep is gonna be such a big one and that's going to depend on where you're at with your testosterone, your estradiol, and progesterone.” Taylor (2026-05-25; reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e @ 30:46; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/reader-mailbag-3-traveling-glutathione-sleep-more-hunter-taylor-williams--5863331e/): “DSEP isn't going help you fall asleep, it's just gonna help stay in your deep sleep realm longer.”
